strategy: ⚠️ Options Too Expensive for $700 Budget — Stock Position or Skip Options

wheelStrategy.ineligibleReason: Stock price $1,082.83 — 1 CSP contract requires ~$108,283 cash. Completely outside a standard retail portfolio capacity. Stock position only; no options strategy viable within budget.

recommendation.title: REIT Compounder — Bullish but Options Cost Prohibitive at $700 Budget
recommendation.entryNote: BUDGET MISMATCH: At $1,082/share, even a tight bull call spread ($1,100/$1,200) costs $3,500–5,000+ per spread — 5–7x the $700 guideline. This stock is best approached as a direct equity position (buy shares) rather than options. If options are used, size to 1 contract maximum with a very wide spread to reduce net debit. IV is low (~22–28% for a REIT) which makes premium relatively fair, but the absolute dollar amount is prohibitive. Next earnings: July 29, 2026 — no IV crush risk in the near term. Q1 2026 was clean: revenue $2.4B +8% YoY, record AI bookings, guidance raised to $10.14–$10.24B. Stock is 4% off its 52-week high. Consider as a core equity hold rather than a leveraged options play.

exitPlan.label: Stock exit 1
exitPlan.trigger: Stock at $1,150 (+6%) on continued record AI bookings
exitPlan.optionGain: ~60–80% on call premium if using options

exitPlan.label: Stock exit 2
exitPlan.trigger: Stock at $1,200–$1,350 (+11–25%) on analyst target upgrades (Citizens PT: $1,350)
exitPlan.optionGain: ~150–300% on call if held to expiry

companyOverview: Equinix is the world's largest data centre REIT and colocation provider, operating 260+ data centres across 71 metros globally. It benefits directly from AI infrastructure buildout: hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, GCP) and enterprises need neutral interconnection hubs to route AI traffic efficiently, and Equinix's IBX data centres are the industry standard. Q1 2026 results were strong: $2.4B revenue (+8% YoY), record gross bookings driven by AI demand, AFFO growing faster than revenue. Full-year 2026 guidance raised to $10.14–$10.24B. The stock is trading near its 52-week high of $1,128 and analysts have recently raised targets significantly: Citizens $1,350, Oppenheimer $1,200, Scotiabank $1,120. The REIT structure provides dividend income alongside capital appreciation.

financials.note: Slight miss vs. $4.30 estimate — non-material; bookings record broke the headline
Q2 2026 — no near-term IV crush risk; clean entry window
Low for a REIT — premium is fair but absolute cost is high
AFFO growth faster than revenue; dividend sustainability strong

insiderFlow.summary: Net SELLING under 10b5-1 plans — no open-market buying. All transactions appear pre-scheduled. Not alarming, but zero conviction buying from insiders.

transactions.note: Pre-planned 10b5-1 sale. CEO-scale holding but all scheduled.
Coordinated 10b5-1 selling across 4 executives on same date — standard diversification.

insiderFlow.signal: NEUTRAL — all sales pre-planned via 10b5-1. No discretionary selling signal. No buying. Insiders own only 0.27% of stock — limited stake.

catalysts: AI interconnection demand: every hyperscaler AI cluster requires Equinix IBX hubs for low-latency data routing — structural demand not cyclical
Record Q1 2026 gross bookings — AI customers signing multi-year contracts; revenue visibility extending into 2028+
FY2026 guidance raised: $10.14–$10.24B confirms management confidence in demand pipeline
Analyst upgrade cycle: Citizens raised to $1,350, Oppenheimer to $1,200 — target revision trend is upward
AFFO growing faster than revenue: bottom line improving as operating leverage kicks in at scale
Global expansion into Asia Pacific and EMEA: new campus builds in markets with undersupplied capacity
risks: Options budget mismatch: $1,082 stock price makes options prohibitively expensive for a $700/trade budget. Use stock position instead.
Q1 EPS slight miss ($4.22 vs $4.30) — if this trend continues, premium REIT valuation (~28x AFFO) becomes harder to justify
High absolute valuation: REIT paying premium to NAV; rising interest rates could compress REIT multiples
All insider selling under 10b5-1 — no conviction buying despite elevated price; 0.27% insider ownership is low
Capex intensity: data centre construction is capital-heavy; rising construction costs and interest rates compress returns
July 29 earnings: if Q2 bookings decelerate from the Q1 record, multiple compression likely
dilution: Typical REIT capital structure. Periodic equity offerings to fund development pipeline. Not an acute dilution concern but watch equity issuance for new campus funding.
